text: I worry a lot.
I worry about our natural world, about having any wilderness left.
And especially about having people wanting to travel here.
We don't seem to have the passion for reconnecting to wild areas like we once did.
We don't seem to have the passion for reconnecting to wild areas like we once did.
What Leopold once said,
The richest values of wilderness lie not in the days of Daniel Boone, nor even in the present, but rather in the future.
He has a good point.
It seems nowadays we have more people trying to survive out here, rather than be part of it.
Maybe I worry too much. My daughter thinks I do.
She enjoys being out here just as much as I do. Probably more.
She memorized the Chief Seattle Quote while on a canoe trip last summer.
She's been looking for a place to live.
Because what it said meant so much to her.
She's been looking for a place to live.
We are part of the earth and it's part of us.
The flowers are our sisters, the deer, the horse, the great eagle.
These are our brothers.
The rocky crests, the juices of the meadows, the body heat of the pony and the man, we all belong to the same family.
The shining waters that move through the streams and rivers is not just water, but the blood of our ancestors.
She's right. I worry too much.
The
last one.
Do aster.
